title: This Massachusetts commute ranks as the most stressful, poll finds
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: The Zakim Bridge/I-93 North in Boston is ranked as the most stressful commute in Massachusetts, according to a Munley Law poll.
The Southeast Expressway and I-95/Route 128 in Waltham are also listed as top stress-inducing commutes in Massachusetts.
The study highlights I-95 as a major source of frustration in New England, affecting drivers in several states.

title: Jaylen Brown describes ‘surreal’ experience at his first St. Patrick’s Day parade in South Boston
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown attended his first St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston, streaming the event on Twitch.
Brown enjoyed interacting with fans, receiving chants at the parade similar to those at Celtics games.
Despite being in Boston for a decade, it was his first parade experience, which he found surreal and enjoyable.

title: Suspected mortar shell brought into Essex police station prompts evacuation
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An Essex police station was evacuated after a resident brought in a suspected mortar shell found on their property.
The Massachusetts State Police Bomb Squad determined the device was a non-explosive, empty canister resembling a mortar shell.
Essex Police urge the public to call 911 and not transport potential explosives, emphasizing public safety precautions.

title: Snag one-way flights for as low as $59 on Southwest
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Southwest Airlines offers one-way flights starting at $59 from March 17-19 for travel between April 7 and June 10.
The sale is valid for flights within the continental US and inter-island Hawaii travel on Tuesdays and Wednesdays.
Boston Logan deals include $59 to Baltimore, $94 to Cleveland, $109 to Savannah, and $160 to San Diego.

title: City councilors call on Boston police to release footage of fatal shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston city councilors Miniard Culpepper and Brian Worrell request police release body camera footage of a fatal shooting in Roxbury.
Stephenson King, 39, was shot by Boston police during a carjacking incident near 1500 Tremont St. on March 11.
Suffolk District Attorney's office is investigating; councilors stress transparency to maintain public trust and officer accountability.

title: JPMorganChase inks deal to become the main tenant of new South Station Tower
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: JPMorganChase will move its Boston offices to South Station Tower in 2028, occupying 250,000 square feet.
The move is part of an expansion plan, including opening a 100th branch in Massachusetts and adding 300 jobs.
South Station Tower features 680,000 square feet of office space and includes tenants like Citadel and Jones Day.

title: Here’s the versatile wide receiver Mel Kiper has the Patriots picking 31st overall in his latest mock draft
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Mel Kiper Jr.'s latest mock draft projects the Patriots to select wide receiver K.C. Concepcion 31st overall.
Concepcion totaled 61 catches, 919 yards, and 9 touchdowns in 2025 at Texas A&M.
Kiper highlights Concepcion's field-stretching ability and impact in the return game.

title: ‘It feels like old times’: As Jayson Tatum’s minutes ramp up, so does chemistry with Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum's return strengthens Celtics chemistry with Jaylen Brown ahead of playoffs.
Brown leads with career-high performance, scoring 41 points in win over Phoenix.
Tatum increases minutes, playing 32 minutes in recent games after Achilles recovery.

title: Mass. man gets 10 years for child porn possession after Google reports him
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Scott Morrill, 52, from Uxbridge, sentenced to 10 years for possessing child porn after Google report.
Google reported child abuse materials in Morrill's online account, leading to his arrest in April 2025.
Morrill was on supervised release for a previous 2013 conviction for distributing child pornography.

title: Local obituary: Matthew McTiernan, 22, Marshfield native and Johnson & Wales student
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Matthew McTiernan, 22, a Marshfield native and Johnson & Wales student, died in a car accident on March 11, 2026.
He was a baking and pastry arts student, known for his humor and creativity, especially in making sourdough bread.
A memorial service is scheduled for March 15, 2026, at North River Community Church in Pembroke, Massachusetts.
